tagline: Survivorship OS for community oncology — breast-first
problem: 18.6M US cancer survivors; treatment ends but symptoms, fear, and care gaps persist
~59% moderate fear of recurrence; breast survivorship: menopause, sexual health, fatigue often unaddressed
SCPs are required but rarely drive behavior change; oncology is optimized for active treatment
solution: Productized survivorship service line embedded in the oncology practice
Digital pathways + NP/clinical team + escalation — not a content super-app
Wedge: HR+/HER2- breast — endocrine side effects, FCR, care coordination
Expand tumor types only after measurable outcomes at anchor sites
market: TAM: 18.6M all US cancer survivors x $400-$900/yr CPT-backed services = $7.4B-$16.7B
CPT stack (Medicare 2024): G0023 PIN $79/mo + 99490 CCM $60 + 99457 RPM $48 + 99484 BHI $48
Gross billable up to ~$2,800/yr/survivor if full stack; TAM uses blended capture
SOM: 250k-600k lives x $180-$260 platform PMPM = $45M-$156M ARR (5 yr)
Beachhead: 4.3M US breast survivors — not TAM denominator
product: Enroll post-treatment cohorts at practice discharge
PRO-based tracking: fatigue, sleep, sexual health, distress, FCR
Protocolized interventions + NP escalation; EHR-aware reporting
Outcomes + utilization dashboard for value-based contracts
traction: Pre-seed: diligence-backed wedge + clinical advisory in formation
Target: 2 design-partner LOIs (community oncology) in 90 days
6-month HR+ breast pilot: symptom burden + engagement endpoints
Series A bar: 3 sites, cohort outcomes, embedded distribution CAC
business_model: Primary: PMPM from practices / risk entities ($15-$25 target)
Secondary: shared savings / quality where contracts allow
Not primary: consumer DTC subscription at scale
Target 70%+ gross margin; CAC via practice-embedded distribution

our_advantages: Only quadrant: practice-embedded + post-treatment survivorship OS
Breast HR+ protocols + CPT stack (PIN/CCM/RPM) — not content or chemo nav
Outcomes + utilization data moat vs OncoveryCare point solution
competitors: X-axis: DTC/employer vs oncology practice & VBC
Y-axis: treatment navigation vs post-treatment survivorship

team: Clinical: [Advisor] — breast oncologist or survivorship NP
financials: 18 mo: 3 live sites, HR+ pilot outcomes, ARR path to Series A
